item.title: Building a Rails Api
item.description: My plan is to build a Rails API that can be hooked up to a front end like React. Project Idea The goal is to build a flashcard app that makes use of spaced repetition.

item.title: The Pyramid Problem
item.description: Directions Write a function that accepts a positive number n. The function should console log a pyramid shape with n levels using the # character. Make sure the pyramid has spaces on both the left and right hand sides.

item.title: How to Check if a String Is a Palindrome in Javascript
item.description: This problem and other common coding interview questions (along with their solutions) can be found in this Github Repo Instructions: If you’re given a string, return true if the string is a palindrome and false if it isn’t.

item.title: How to Reverse a String in Javascript
item.description: Note: This problem and other common coding interview questions (along with their solutions) can be found in this Github Repo Instructions: Take a string and return a new string with a reversed order of characters.

item.title: Working With UseState
item.description: React With Hooks What is the hooks system? It’s a feature added by the React team that gives functional components the ability to use React state and lifecycle methods. There are built-in hooks like useState and useEffect, but it is also possible to create a hook of your own design.

item.title: Creating a Video Search App
item.description: Before we begin: There is a working version of this app hosted here and you can view the complete Github Repository here. In this post I’m going to walkthrough how to build a simple React app that uses the YouTube API to:

item.title: Working With Forms and External APIs
item.description: In most apps that you might work on you’re going to have to be able to accept inputs from users and then use those inputs to do something. This application will demonstrate how to use a controlled form to accept inputs from a user and make a fetch request to an external API.

item.title: Working With Class Based Components
item.description: Note: I built a little app to demonstrate how to work with class-based components. You can view the repository here: Github Repo React has evolved through the years, and as a result functional components are much more powerful than they used to be.

item.title: React Props: A Primer
item.description: In React, components make it really easy to duplicate parts of a website with slight variations. For example, say you wanted to have a comment section underneath your blog posts. Each comment would look very similar, with an avatar, a date, a text block, and maybe even some like and dislike buttons.

item.title: JSX: A Few Notes
item.description: JSX is getting translated into JavaScript, but it isn’t a one-to-one translation. There are a few syntactical notes to be aware of. Inline Styling In HTML, inline styling is done using the “style” attribute and giving it a string of CSS.

item.title: How to Generate a React App
item.description: This is really simple. Make sure that you have node installed, and that it’s a modern enough version of node to include npx. Then, simply type the command npx create-react-app my-new-app into the terminal.
